title: podman image scp remote support & podman image scp tagging
body:
add support for podman-remote image scp as well as direct access via the API. This entailed
a full rework of the layering of image scp functions as well as the usual API plugging and type creation

also, implemented podman image scp tagging. which makes the syntax much more readable and allows users t tag the new image
they are loading to the local/remote machine:

allow users to pass a "new name" for the image they are transferring
`podman tag` as implemented creates a new image im `image list` when tagging, so this does the same
meaning that when transferring images with tags, podman on the remote machine/user will load two images
ex: `podman image scp computer1::alpine computer2::foobar` creates alpine:latest and localhost/foobar on the remote host

implementing tags means removal of the buggy syntax. In the currently released podman image scp, the user can either specify
`podman image scp source::img dest::` or `podman image scp dest:: source::img`. However, with tags this task becomes really hard to check
which is the image (src) and which is the new tag (dst). Removal of that streamlines the arg parsing process

Signed-off-by: cdoern <cbdoer23@g.holycross.edu>

<!--

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
podman image scp API support added as well as image tagging. This fixes a bug in the syntax allowing users to specify a destination before a source. The API endpoint takes a source, destination, and quiet boolean all in the query itself.

```

comments.body: @jwhonce PTAL
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

@cdoern could I encourage you to rebase onto main any time you do a new push?
```console
$ git checkout main
$ git pull -r (or however you update main)
$ git checkout my-branch
$ git rebase main
! NOW you can git-push-force!
```
Advantages:
* You pick up the latest flake fixes, which means
  * you and everyone waste less time pressing Re-run
  * /me wastes less time looking at flake logs, wondering why this-or-that flake is recurring after being fixed a week ago
* and, there is less likelihood of a future collision. (Not all collisions cause merge conflicts. Some collisions happen because of changes in functionality, not blocks of code. These cannot be detected automatically). The closer your PR is to current main, the less chance there is of that happening.

Thank you!

sure @edsantiago sorry for the inconvenience. I usually do:
`git checkout <my branch>`
`git fetch upstream main`
`git rebase upstream/main`
`git push -f origin <my branch>`

This has the same effect I think right?

Hmmm, it should, but if you look at your git tree you'll see that this PR is coming from a72b2402c which is a week old. Yet you pushed it 3 hours ago. Did you not follow your usual process? You might want to look at those step-by-step and see where the breakdown is. Thanks again!
One nit about file structure, but overall LGTM
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

@edsantiago any idea why the change you made: adding `stop_registry` to https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/bce97a3b5dd1477c74232b49d246491aa289a995/test/apiv2/12-imagesMore.at seem to be breaking the API v2 tests here?

edit: it might actually be 10-images that timing out...
@cdoern no idea, and no way to find out without the (hidden by default) logs. I'm trying to reproduce locally. (All passed on my first try just now).

> @cdoern no idea, and no way to find out without the (hidden by default) logs. I'm trying to reproduce locally. (All passed on my first try just now).

let me try a rebase, I thought I did but I will try again

@cdoern followup: I can reproduce this in CI using the "rerun terminal" trick that @cevich demoed yesterday (THANK YOU @cevich!). I cannot understand what is going on. This has been the only thing I've worked on this morning, it is my entire focus (except for two minutes on a different PR review). I am baffled, but I am continuing to focus 100% on it. Will keep you posted.
thanks for the update @edsantiago !!! I @ you in another PR (#14299) where the same thing is happening. Let me know if I can help in any way

@jwhonce made most of your edits. the stdin problem is a non issue in the remote as the command for inter user transfer is the only place where stdin in queried (for sudo password if needed) and that functionality is not allowed in remote
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL and merge if possible
@jwhonce @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL. I have other scp issues and commits I need to work on after this merges.
